sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Multimorbidity: clinical assessment and management — Recommendations
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE geriatric and primary-care adaptation
sources.population: Adults with two or more long-term conditions, including frailty, falls, sensory impairment, chronic pain, mental health conditions, and treatment burden.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary and community care with person-centred multimorbidity review.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for documenting day-to-day function, frailty and falls context, treatment burden, multiple medicines, personal goals, values, carer involvement, and clinician-led treatment review. It does not auto-score frailty or recommend starting, stopping, or changing treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.4.4 — multimorbidity, daily activities, falls, medicines, and frailty context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting long-term-condition burden, day-to-day activity difficulty, support from multiple services, frailty or falls context, number of regular medicines, gait and self-reported health assessment when actually completed.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.8 — treatment burden, day-to-day life, carers, goals, values, and priorities
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting functional impact, mental and physical health interaction, treatment burden, carer involvement, independence, social activity, personal goals, values, priorities, and medicine concerns.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.6.9–1.6.15 — medicines and treatment review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led review of benefits, harms, treatment burden, personal priorities, and a documented discussion about reducing or stopping treatment without generating a medication change.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.clinical_setting: Hospital, community, and long-term stroke rehabilitation and review.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for adult stroke rehabilitation assessment and longer-term review; not an exact source for isolated TIA follow-up and not directly validated for UAE services.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.14 — stroke assessment and rehabilitation plan
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports cognition, vision, hearing, swallowing, movement, continence, communication, nutrition, strength, sensation, balance, mood and mental health, pain, activity, participation, environment, medicines, allergies, goals, discharge information, and documented follow-up planning.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.17, 1.4.3, and 1.17.1–1.17.5 — transfer and long-term review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports post-transfer follow-up, information needs, daily activities, participation goals, work, driving and transport discussion, and review of health and social care needs at 6 months and annually.
